title: Gas meter displaying zero usage after firmware v3.2 update
description: Customer reported that after the scheduled firmware upgrade to v3.2, the gas meter stopped recording any usage. Display shows 0.00 m³ continuously. Manual reading confirms gas is being consumed. Meter model: Itron G4-RF.
root_cause: Firmware v3.2 had a regression bug in the pulse counter module causing it to reset to zero after reboot. The metrology register was not persisting across firmware flash cycles.
resolution: Rolled back firmware to v3.1.4. Applied hotfix patch v3.2.1 which addressed the pulse counter persistence issue. Recalibrated meter and verified readings against manual check for 48 hours.

title: Multiple gas meters not updating after firmware rollout in Dallas region
description: Batch firmware update pushed to 340 meters in Dallas. 47 meters stopped reporting usage data. All affected meters are Itron G4-RF with hardware revision B. Meters with hardware revision C are unaffected.
root_cause: Firmware v3.2 incompatibility with hardware revision B of Itron G4-RF. The memory mapping for metrology data differs between rev B and rev C. Firmware was only tested on rev C hardware.
resolution: Emergency rollback of firmware on all rev B meters. Engineering team created hardware-specific firmware build v3.2-B. Staged rollout with 10% canary deployment before full push. Added hardware revision check to firmware deployment pipeline.

title: Customer billing shows estimated reading instead of actual
description: Customer complaint about receiving estimated bills for 3 consecutive months despite having a smart meter installed. AMI head-end shows last successful read was 90 days ago.
root_cause: RF communication module antenna connector was loose, causing intermittent signal loss. The meter was physically recording usage correctly but failing to transmit readings to the AMI network.
resolution: Field technician tightened the antenna connector and replaced the weatherproofing seal. Verified 3 successful remote reads over 72 hours. Rebilled customer with actual readings for the estimated period.

title: SCADA system showing stale pressure readings on pipeline segment P-441
description: Control room operator noticed that pressure readings for pipeline segment P-441 have been showing the same value (42.3 PSI) for the last 6 hours. Adjacent segments are showing normal fluctuations. No alarms triggered.
root_cause: RTU (Remote Terminal Unit) at monitoring station P-441-RTU had a frozen watchdog timer. The RTU was still responding to polls but serving cached data from its last valid read. The pressure transducer was functioning correctly.
resolution: Remote restart of RTU via SCADA command. Updated RTU firmware to include stale-data detection (if reading unchanged for >2 hours, trigger alert). Added automated stale-data alarm rule in SCADA historian.

title: Electric meter reading spike - 10x normal consumption reported
description: Customer flagged abnormally high bill. Meter ID EM-44921 reported 4,200 kWh for the month vs normal 420 kWh average. Customer is a single-family residence with no changes to appliances or usage pattern.
root_cause: CT (Current Transformer) ratio was misconfigured during a recent meter swap. The multiplier was set to 10x instead of 1x in the meter programming. Physical meter was recording correctly but the head-end system was applying the wrong multiplier.
resolution: Corrected CT ratio in meter configuration from 10:1 to 1:1. Recalculated billing for affected period. Issued credit to customer account. Added CT ratio validation step to meter swap checklist.

title: Pipeline pressure drop alarm - segment P-227 compressor station
description: Automated alarm triggered for pressure drop exceeding 15% on pipeline segment P-227 near Midland compressor station. Current pressure: 580 PSI. Normal operating range: 680-720 PSI. No visible leak detected by aerial survey.
root_cause: Compressor station bypass valve was partially open due to a faulty actuator solenoid. Gas was recirculating through the bypass instead of being fully compressed into the pipeline. No leak present.
resolution: Replaced faulty solenoid actuator on bypass valve. Pressure restored to 695 PSI within 2 hours. Implemented monthly actuator position verification in maintenance schedule. Added bypass valve position to SCADA monitoring dashboard.

title: Smart meter not connecting to WiFi HAN after router change
description: Customer replaced home router. Smart meter HAN (Home Area Network) interface no longer communicating with in-home display (IHD). Meter is still reporting to utility via RF mesh. Only local HAN display is affected.
root_cause: Customer's new router uses WPA3 only. The meter's HAN module only supports WPA2. The IHD was previously connected via the old router's WPA2 network.
resolution: Guided customer to enable WPA2/WPA3 mixed mode on their router settings. Re-paired IHD with meter HAN. Verified real-time usage display working. Documented WPA3 incompatibility for future reference.

title: Gas leak detection sensor false alarms at regulator station RS-18
description: Methane detection sensor at regulator station RS-18 has triggered 12 false alarms in the past week. Each alarm requires emergency response team dispatch. No actual gas leak confirmed on any occasion. Sensor model: MSA Ultima X5000.
root_cause: Sensor calibration drift due to exposure to high humidity and temperature cycling. The electrochemical cell had degraded beyond its calibration tolerance, causing false positive readings at 0.8-1.2% LEL (Lower Explosive Limit).
resolution: Replaced electrochemical sensor cell. Performed full calibration with certified test gas. Installed environmental housing with humidity control. Set up quarterly calibration schedule instead of annual. Added sensor health monitoring to predictive maintenance dashboard.

title: AMI head-end server performance degradation causing delayed meter reads
description: AMI head-end processing time increased from average 2 minutes to 45 minutes per batch. Approximately 50,000 meter reads are being delayed daily. No infrastructure changes made recently. Server CPU at 95% utilization.
root_cause: Database index fragmentation reached 98% on the meter_reads table after 6 months without maintenance. Additionally, a scheduled report query was running during peak ingestion hours, causing table locks.
resolution: Rebuilt database indexes on meter_reads and related tables. Rescheduled report generation to off-peak hours (2 AM - 4 AM). Implemented automated index maintenance job weekly. Added database performance monitoring alerts at 70% CPU threshold.

title: Cathodic protection system voltage drop on pipeline segment P-612
description: Routine inspection revealed cathodic protection (CP) voltage on segment P-612 dropped from -1.2V to -0.6V (below minimum -0.85V protection threshold). Pipeline at risk of corrosion if not addressed within regulatory timeframe.
root_cause: Rectifier unit transformer had a partial winding failure reducing output capacity by 50%. The anode bed resistance had also increased due to soil moisture changes from recent drought conditions.
resolution: Replaced rectifier transformer. Installed supplemental anode bed to compensate for increased soil resistance. Verified CP voltage restored to -1.15V across full segment. Filed compliance report with PHMSA. Scheduled quarterly CP survey instead of annual.

title: Customer reports gas smell near outdoor meter set
description: Residential customer called emergency line reporting gas odor near their outdoor meter set. No visible damage to meter or piping. Odor is intermittent. Gas detector shows trace readings of 0.3% LEL at meter connection point.
root_cause: Hairline crack in the meter set riser pipe at the threaded connection point. The crack was caused by ground settling that put lateral stress on the riser. Leak was small enough to be intermittent based on pressure fluctuations.
resolution: Isolated meter and replaced riser pipe with flexible connector to accommodate ground movement. Pressure tested new installation at 1.5x operating pressure. Cleared area with combustible gas detector. Restored service after 24-hour leak check.

title: Incorrect tariff code applied to commercial gas customer
description: Commercial bakery customer noticed billing rate doesn't match their contract. They're being billed at residential rate (Rate R) instead of commercial small business rate (Rate CS). Been overbilled for approximately 4 months.
root_cause: During CIS (Customer Information System) migration, the tariff mapping table had an error where commercial accounts with usage below 500 therms/month were incorrectly classified as residential. Affected 23 commercial accounts.
resolution: Corrected tariff mapping in CIS. Identified all 23 affected accounts. Recalculated bills for the 4-month period. Issued credits totaling $12,400 across affected accounts. Added tariff validation rule to prevent misclassification.

title: SCADA communication loss to 8 remote sites after network switch replacement
description: Planned network switch replacement at regional operations center resulted in loss of SCADA communication to 8 remote monitoring sites. Sites are operating in autonomous mode with local safety controls active. No manual override capability from control room.
root_cause: New network switch had different VLAN tagging behavior. The SCADA traffic VLAN (VLAN 100) was configured as tagged on the new switch but the RTU network interfaces expected untagged traffic. Additionally, spanning tree protocol reconvergence caused a 45-minute network loop.
resolution: Configured VLAN 100 as native/untagged on RTU-facing ports. Disabled spanning tree on dedicated SCADA network segments. Restored communication to all 8 sites within 4 hours. Implemented pre-change network validation checklist. Added SCADA communication monitoring with 5-minute heartbeat alerts.

title: Electric meter time-of-use (TOU) register not switching at correct times
description: TOU electric meter for commercial customer showing all usage in off-peak register. Peak hours should be 2 PM - 7 PM weekdays. Customer is a manufacturing plant that operates primarily during peak hours.
root_cause: Meter internal clock had drifted by 8 hours due to a depleted backup battery. When primary power was momentarily lost during a brief outage, the clock reset to UTC instead of local time (CST/CDT). TOU schedule was operating on UTC time.
resolution: Replaced meter backup battery. Synchronized meter clock to NTP server via AMI network. Verified TOU register switching at correct local times for 1 week. Recalculated customer bills for affected period using load profile data. Implemented weekly clock sync check in AMI system.

title: SCADA historian database running out of storage
description: SCADA historian server storage at 94% capacity. At current ingestion rate, storage will be exhausted in approximately 12 days. Historian stores 2 years of operational data for 15,000 data points at 1-second resolution.
root_cause: A misconfigured data collection rule was polling 3,000 analog points at 100ms intervals instead of the intended 1-second intervals, generating 10x the expected data volume. The misconfiguration occurred 3 weeks ago during a polling rate optimization project.
resolution: Corrected polling interval to 1 second for affected data points. Archived data older than 18 months to cold storage. Compressed historical data using lossy compression for points older than 6 months. Added storage capacity alerts at 75% and 85% thresholds. Implemented polling rate change audit log.

title: Gas odorization level below regulatory minimum at distribution point DP-33
description: Routine sniff test at distribution point DP-33 showed odorant level below the 1/5 LEL detection threshold required by 49 CFR 192.625. Last odorant injection system check was 30 days ago and was within spec.
root_cause: Odorant injection pump diaphragm had developed a micro-tear, reducing output by approximately 40%. The reduced injection rate combined with higher than normal gas flow (due to cold weather demand spike) resulted in below-threshold odorant concentration.
resolution: Replaced odorant injection pump diaphragm immediately. Increased injection rate temporarily to compensate. Verified odorant levels at multiple downstream test points. Filed regulatory notification with Texas Railroad Commission. Implemented weekly pump output verification during winter demand season.

title: Prepaid electric meter not deducting balance correctly
description: Prepaid customer reports their balance is decreasing faster than expected. Meter shows 3x the consumption compared to same period last year. No new appliances added. Neighbor with similar home size uses significantly less.
root_cause: Neutral wire connection at the meter base was corroded, causing current to return through ground path. This resulted in unbalanced CT readings where one phase was reading the sum of two phases. Actual consumption was normal but meter was recording approximately 2.8x actual usage.
resolution: Replaced corroded neutral connection and meter base. Installed new meter with verified calibration. Tested with known load to confirm accuracy. Credited customer for overbilled amount ($340). Added neutral integrity check to meter installation checklist.

title: Automated meter reading (AMR) drive-by route failing for 120 meters
description: AMR drive-by route 47 consistently failing to collect reads from approximately 120 out of 800 meters on the route. Failed meters are clustered in a new subdivision with metal-clad homes.
root_cause: Metal cladding on homes in the new subdivision was attenuating RF signals from the ERT (Encoder Receiver Transmitter) modules. Signal strength at the street was below the -85 dBm minimum threshold for the mobile collector.
resolution: Installed high-gain antennas on affected ERTs. Adjusted drive-by route to include a slow-pass through the subdivision. For 15 meters with persistent issues, installed repeater nodes on utility poles. Achieved 99.2% read rate on next collection pass.

title: Power transformer oil temperature alarm at substation SS-14
description: Oil temperature on 50 MVA power transformer at substation SS-14 reached 92°C against alarm threshold of 90°C. Load is at 78% capacity. Ambient temperature is 38°C. Cooling fans are running.
root_cause: Two of six cooling fan motors had failed (bearings seized). With only 4 of 6 fans operational, cooling capacity was insufficient for the current load and ambient temperature combination. Oil quality test showed acceptable dissolved gas levels.
resolution: Reduced load to 60% by switching feeders to adjacent transformer. Replaced both failed fan motors. Verified all 6 fans operational. Restored normal loading. Oil temperature stabilized at 78°C. Scheduled vibration analysis for remaining fan motors. Added fan motor current monitoring to predictive maintenance system.

title: Gas meter register reading mismatch between physical and remote
description: Field technician noticed physical meter index reads 45,678 therms but AMI system shows 44,123 therms. Discrepancy of 1,555 therms. Meter has been in service for 3 years with no previous discrepancies.
root_cause: ERT module encoder wheel had slipped by 2 positions during a recent storm with high winds that physically shook the meter. The encoder was mechanically coupled but not locked, causing it to lose count synchronization with the meter register.
resolution: Re-synchronized ERT encoder with physical meter register. Applied mechanical locking compound to encoder coupling. Adjusted AMI reading to match physical register. Backdated correction in billing system. Added encoder alignment check to annual meter inspection procedure.

title: Distributed Energy Resource (DER) solar inverter causing voltage fluctuations
description: Multiple customers on feeder F-221 reporting flickering lights and voltage fluctuations between 114V and 126V. Feeder has 12 residential solar installations totaling 85 kW. Fluctuations correlate with cloud cover patterns.
root_cause: Rapid solar output changes during partly cloudy conditions causing voltage swings that exceeded the ±5% ANSI C84.1 standard. The feeder voltage regulator had a 60-second response time, too slow for the rapid DER output changes.
resolution: Upgraded feeder voltage regulator to fast-acting electronic type with 2-second response time. Configured smart inverter Volt-VAR settings on the 3 largest solar installations (per IEEE 1547-2018). Installed capacitor bank for reactive power support. Voltage now maintained within ±3%.

title: Electric meter showing negative consumption readings
description: Residential customer's smart meter reporting negative kWh readings intermittently. Customer does not have solar panels or any generation source. Net meter reading is still positive but individual interval data shows negative values.
root_cause: Meter CT polarity was reversed on Phase B during installation. When loads are primarily on Phase B, the meter records negative values. Overall reading appears roughly correct because Phase A compensates, but interval data is corrupted.
resolution: Swapped CT leads on Phase B to correct polarity. Verified correct reading direction on all phases with known test load. Recalculated interval data for billing period using load profile estimation. Updated meter installation verification procedure to include per-phase polarity check.

title: Duplicate billing for gas service at same address
description: Customer receiving two separate gas bills for the same service address. One bill is for their current account and another appears to be for a previous tenant's account that was never closed. Total charges are double actual usage.
root_cause: During the move-in process, the previous tenant's account was not properly closed in the CIS. The meter was associated with both accounts simultaneously, causing usage to be billed to both. The old account had defaulted to paper billing, so the previous tenant never noticed.
resolution: Closed the previous tenant's account. Issued refund for the duplicate charges. Reconciled meter readings between both accounts. Added validation rule in CIS to prevent multiple active accounts on same meter. Implemented automated detection of dual-billing scenarios.

title: Pipeline integrity management - ILI pig stuck in segment P-305
description: In-line inspection (ILI) pig launched for integrity assessment became stuck at mile marker 34.2 of pipeline segment P-305. Pipeline flow reduced by 60%. Pig was a 16-inch MFL (Magnetic Flux Leakage) tool.
root_cause: Pipe wall deformation (dent) at mile marker 34.2 reduced internal diameter below the pig's minimum passage requirement. The dent was caused by third-party excavation activity 6 months ago that was not reported or detected during routine patrol.
resolution: Reduced pipeline pressure and used bypass to push pig through dent with controlled pressure differential. Retrieved pig at receiving station. Excavated and assessed dent per ASME B31.8. Installed composite repair sleeve. Updated GIS with dent location. Enhanced third-party damage prevention program with additional patrol frequency.

title: Above-ground gas pipe corrosion at industrial customer site
description: Annual inspection found visible corrosion and coating failure on 2-inch above-ground gas piping at industrial customer's meter set. Pipe wall thickness measurement shows 15% wall loss. Pipe is in a chemical processing area with corrosive atmosphere.
root_cause: Original protective coating was standard epoxy not rated for chemical exposure. Hydrogen sulfide and chlorine compounds in the facility's atmosphere degraded the coating within 2 years. Accelerated corrosion followed coating failure.
resolution: Replaced affected pipe section with Schedule 80 stainless steel. Applied chemical-resistant polyurethane coating. Installed sacrificial zinc anodes for additional cathodic protection. Updated material specification for industrial installations in corrosive environments. Scheduled 6-month inspection cycle for this site.

title: Net metering credits not applied to customer bill
description: Solar customer with net metering agreement reports that excess generation credits are not appearing on their monthly bill. Meter shows bidirectional readings. Customer has been exporting average 200 kWh/month for 6 months.
root_cause: The billing system rate engine did not have the net metering credit rule activated for this customer's account. During enrollment, the rate code was updated but the credit calculation module was not enabled. This was a manual step that was missed.
resolution: Activated net metering credit calculation for the account. Retroactively calculated and applied credits for all 6 months ($186 total credit). Automated the credit module activation as part of net metering enrollment workflow. Audited all 450 net metering accounts to verify credit status.

title: Cybersecurity alert - unauthorized SCADA login attempt detected
description: Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system flagged 47 failed login attempts to SCADA HMI server from an internal IP address (10.45.2.108) over a 15-minute period. The IP belongs to a contractor workstation in the engineering office.
root_cause: A contractor's laptop had been infected with credential-harvesting malware through a phishing email. The malware was attempting to brute-force the SCADA HMI login using a dictionary attack. No successful access was achieved due to account lockout policy (5 attempts).
resolution: Isolated contractor workstation from network immediately. Performed forensic analysis. Cleaned malware and reimaged laptop. Reset all SCADA user credentials. Implemented multi-factor authentication for SCADA access. Added contractor network segmentation with restricted SCADA access. Conducted security awareness training for all contractors.

title: Gas compressor station vibration alarm - unit CS-7B
description: Vibration sensors on gas compressor unit CS-7B at Bear Creek station showing readings of 12 mm/s (alarm threshold: 10 mm/s, trip threshold: 15 mm/s). Vibration increased gradually over past 2 weeks. Unit running at 85% load.
root_cause: Compressor coupling alignment had shifted by 0.008 inches due to thermal growth differential between compressor and driver. Foundation bolt on driver side had loosened, allowing slight movement during thermal cycling.
resolution: Shut down unit and performed laser alignment. Torqued all foundation bolts to specification. Replaced coupling elastomer insert (showing wear from misalignment). Restarted unit - vibration at 3.2 mm/s (well within normal). Implemented monthly alignment trend monitoring. Added thermal growth compensation to alignment procedure.

title: Demand response event failed to curtail load on 200 smart thermostats
description: During peak demand event, demand response signal sent to 500 enrolled smart thermostats. Only 300 responded. 200 thermostats in the Lakewood subdivision did not receive or act on the curtailment signal.
root_cause: The Lakewood subdivision thermostats were connected through a cellular gateway that had exceeded its data plan limit. The demand response commands were queued but not delivered. The gateway had been misconfigured with a consumer data plan instead of an IoT plan.
resolution: Upgraded gateway to unlimited IoT data plan. Implemented store-and-forward capability so commands are delivered when connectivity resumes. Added gateway connectivity monitoring with 1-hour heartbeat check. Tested demand response on all 500 thermostats successfully. Added pre-event connectivity verification step to demand response protocol.

title: Gas chromatograph at city gate station giving erroneous BTU readings
description: Gas chromatograph at city gate station CG-04 reporting BTU values of 1,180 BTU/scf when expected range is 1,020-1,040 BTU/scf. Downstream custody transfer meters using this BTU value for energy calculation. Potential billing impact across 45,000 customers.
root_cause: Sample conditioning system filter was saturated with pipeline liquids (condensate), causing sample gas composition to be enriched with heavier hydrocarbons. The automated filter change alert had been disabled during a maintenance window 2 months ago and never re-enabled.
resolution: Replaced sample conditioning filter immediately. BTU readings returned to 1,032 BTU/scf within 1 hour. Re-enabled all maintenance alerts. Calculated billing impact for the 3 days of erroneous readings. Applied corrections to custody transfer calculations. Implemented redundant BTU measurement with cross-validation alarm.

title: Customer smart meter data showing in wrong account after meter swap
description: After meter swap program, Customer A is seeing Customer B's usage data in their online portal. Customer B is seeing Customer A's data. Both customers are on the same street. Billing appears correct in CIS.
root_cause: During the meter swap, the field technician transposed the meter serial numbers in the work order. Meter M-44521 was installed at Customer A's address but recorded as Customer B's, and vice versa. CIS billed correctly based on physical installation, but the portal pulled data based on the incorrect serial number mapping.
resolution: Corrected meter-to-customer mapping in AMI system. Verified physical meter serial numbers at both locations. Refreshed portal data cache. Confirmed both customers now see their correct usage data. Added barcode scanning requirement to meter swap procedure to prevent transposition errors.

title: Renewable natural gas (RNG) injection pressure exceeding limits
description: RNG injection point at landfill gas processing facility showing injection pressure of 62 PSI against interconnect agreement maximum of 55 PSI. Downstream distribution system rated for 60 PSI MAOP. Safety margin being violated.
root_cause: RNG facility compressor discharge pressure regulator failed in the open position. The over-pressure protection valve (OPPV) setpoint was incorrectly set at 65 PSI instead of the required 55 PSI due to a documentation error in the interconnect commissioning.
resolution: Shut in RNG injection immediately. Replaced failed pressure regulator. Corrected OPPV setpoint to 55 PSI. Installed redundant pressure monitoring with automatic shut-off at 53 PSI. Updated interconnect agreement documentation. Performed compliance audit on all 3 RNG injection points in the system.

title: AMI mesh network coverage gap in rural service area
description: 230 meters in rural area south of Johnson County have less than 60% daily read success rate. AMI mesh network has insufficient density for reliable multi-hop communication. Area has rolling terrain and heavy tree cover.
root_cause: AMI mesh network design assumed flat terrain with line-of-sight between relay points. The rolling hills and mature oak tree canopy attenuated signals below the -95 dBm sensitivity threshold. Relay spacing of 1,200 feet was too far for this terrain.
resolution: Installed 8 additional AMI relay nodes on elevated positions (water towers, utility poles). Deployed 3 cellular gateways for meters in the most challenging locations. Reduced effective relay spacing to 600 feet in hilly areas. Read success rate improved to 97.8%. Updated RF propagation model to account for terrain and vegetation.

title: Gas emergency valve (ESD) failed to close during drill
description: During quarterly emergency shutdown drill at gate station GS-12, the main ESD valve failed to fully close. Valve reached 80% closed position then stalled. Valve is a 12-inch ball valve with pneumatic actuator.
root_cause: Actuator spring pack had lost tension due to metal fatigue. The spring is the fail-safe mechanism that drives the valve closed on loss of pneumatic pressure. Spring had not been replaced in 12 years (recommended lifecycle: 10 years). Partial closure was achieved from remaining pneumatic pressure.
resolution: Replaced actuator spring pack with upgraded stainless steel springs. Performed full stroke test - valve closes in 4.2 seconds (spec: <5 seconds). Updated maintenance schedule to replace springs at 8-year intervals. Implemented annual spring tension testing. Audited all 34 ESD valves in system for spring age.

title: Billing system calculating wrong demand charges for industrial customers
description: Three industrial customers reported incorrect demand charges. System is using 15-minute demand intervals but calculating based on 30-minute averages, resulting in lower demand readings and undercharging. Estimated revenue impact: $23,000/month.
root_cause: A billing system update 2 months ago changed the default demand interval from 15 minutes to 30 minutes for all rate classes. The change was intended only for a new residential rate pilot but was applied globally due to a missing rate class filter in the update script.
resolution: Rolled back demand interval to 15 minutes for all non-pilot rate classes. Recalculated demand charges for all affected industrial accounts for the 2-month period. Issued corrected bills with explanation letters. Implemented rate class-specific configuration with separate update procedures. Added billing validation rules to detect demand calculation anomalies.

title: Electric vehicle charger causing harmonic distortion on residential transformer
description: Transformer T-8841 serving 8 homes has started buzzing audibly. Power quality analysis shows THD (Total Harmonic Distortion) of 12% (limit: 8%). One home recently installed a 48A Level 2 EV charger. Three other homes already had EV chargers.
root_cause: Cumulative harmonic injection from four EV chargers on a single 50 kVA transformer exceeded the transformer's harmonic withstand capability. The newest charger uses an older rectifier design with higher harmonic content than the other three. Combined loading during evening charging (6-10 PM) reaches 92% of transformer rating.
resolution: Installed harmonic filter at transformer secondary. Contacted newest EV charger owner about upgrading to a charger with active power factor correction. Submitted request for transformer upgrade to 75 kVA. Implemented EV charger notification process to track cumulative loading per transformer. THD reduced to 5.2%.

title: Automated gas shut-off valve activated without apparent cause
description: Residential customer's automated earthquake gas shut-off valve (EGSV) activated at 3:47 AM. No seismic activity recorded in the area. Customer woke up to no gas service for heating. Temperature was 28°F outside.
root_cause: Heavy truck traffic from nearby highway construction project generated ground vibrations that exceeded the EGSV's sensitivity threshold (0.3g). The valve was installed closer to the street than recommended (8 feet instead of minimum 15 feet from roadway). Construction activity started 2 weeks ago.
resolution: Reset EGSV and restored gas service within 2 hours. Relocated EGSV to the side of the house furthest from street (22 feet from roadway). Adjusted sensitivity to 0.4g per manufacturer's recommendation for high-vibration environments. Notified 12 other customers with EGSV installations near the construction zone. Coordinated with construction company on vibration monitoring.

title: Underground gas main leak detected by mobile leak survey
description: Mobile leak survey vehicle detected elevated methane readings (Class 2 leak - non-hazardous, scheduled for repair) on Oak Street between blocks 400-500. Readings indicate subsurface gas migration. Main is a 4-inch plastic PE pipe installed in 1998.
root_cause: Electrofusion joint failure at a service tee connection. The joint was improperly fused during original installation - fusion zone showed incomplete melt pattern in cross-section analysis. The leak had been slowly growing for an estimated 2-3 years based on corrosion pattern and soil gas migration extent.
resolution: Excavated and exposed the failed joint. Installed full-encirclement repair clamp as temporary measure. Scheduled permanent repair with new electrofusion coupling. Surveyed all joints on the block - found one additional marginal joint repaired proactively. Updated leak survey frequency for pre-2000 PE installations from annual to semi-annual.

title: SCADA polling timeout on 15 remote sites after firewall rule change
description: After a routine firewall rule update by IT security team, SCADA master station unable to poll 15 remote sites. Sites are operating in local autonomous mode. Firewall change was to block a range of IP addresses flagged by threat intelligence.
root_cause: The blocked IP range (10.88.0.0/16) overlapped with the SCADA RTU subnet (10.88.4.0/24). The firewall rule was applied to the IT/OT boundary firewall without consulting the SCADA network diagram. The threat intelligence IPs were in the 10.88.200.0/24 range but the block was applied to the entire /16.
resolution: Refined firewall rule to block only 10.88.200.0/24 instead of the entire /16. Restored SCADA communication to all 15 sites within 30 minutes of rule correction. Implemented mandatory SCADA team review for any firewall changes affecting OT network ranges. Created a protected IP whitelist for critical SCADA subnets. Documented all SCADA network ranges in firewall change management system.

title: Power outage affecting 2,400 customers - cause unknown
description: Unplanned outage on feeder F-445 at 2:15 PM affecting 2,400 customers. Breaker at substation tripped on overcurrent protection. No storm or weather event. Recloser attempted 3 times before lockout.
root_cause: Mylar balloon contacted overhead primary conductor at pole P-44521, causing a phase-to-phase fault. The balloon's metallic coating created a sustained arc that exceeded the recloser's programmed clear time. Fault current was 8,500A against a 6,000A trip setting.
resolution: Identified fault location using fault circuit indicators. Removed balloon debris and repaired damaged conductor splice. Restored power within 2 hours. Reviewed recloser coordination settings. Added animal/object guard on the three poles near the park where balloon originated. Filed outage report per regulatory requirement.

title: Pipeline right-of-way encroachment - unauthorized construction
description: Aerial patrol detected unauthorized construction (concrete foundation pour) within 10 feet of a 20-inch high-pressure gas transmission pipeline. Location: Latitude 32.7767, Longitude -96.7970. Builder did not call 811 before excavating.
root_cause: Construction company began work without contacting 811 One-Call notification system as required by Texas law. The pipeline was not marked and the builder was unaware of its location. Excavation came within 3 feet of the pipe at its closest point. No pipe damage detected by close-interval survey.
resolution: Issued stop-work order through local authorities. Performed detailed close-interval potential survey confirming no coating or pipe damage. Installed additional marker posts and warning signs. Met with construction company to establish safe setback distances. Filed violation report with Texas Railroad Commission. Conducted pipe-to-soil potential readings to verify cathodic protection integrity.

title: Gas pressure regulator station delivering low pressure to subdivision
description: 15 customers in Elm Creek subdivision reporting low gas pressure. Appliances not functioning properly. Pressure at closest meter is 3.8 inches WC (minimum required: 5.0 inches WC). Regulator station RS-42 serves the area.
root_cause: Regulator diaphragm had developed a small tear, reducing the regulator's ability to maintain setpoint under high demand. Combined with cold weather demand increase, outlet pressure dropped below minimum. The regulator had been in service for 15 years (recommended replacement: 12 years).
resolution: Replaced regulator assembly with new unit. Downstream pressure restored to 6.5 inches WC. Verified pressure at all 15 customer meters. Checked all appliances at the 3 customers who reported issues. Updated regulator replacement schedule based on age analysis. Flagged 8 other regulators past recommended service life for proactive replacement.

title: Smart meter refusing remote disconnect command
description: Meter M-78234 at a vacant property is not responding to remote disconnect command. Multiple attempts over 3 days. Meter is communicating normally for read commands. Property owner requested disconnection during renovation.
root_cause: Meter's internal disconnect switch relay had welded contacts due to an arc event when the meter was previously reconnected under load (the switch was closed while a high-inrush motor was starting). The relay cannot physically open even though the meter acknowledges the disconnect command.
resolution: Dispatched field crew to manually pull meter for disconnection. Replaced meter with new unit (installed in disconnected state). Investigated relay welding - found disconnect command was sent during a period of high load. Updated remote disconnect procedure to check load level before sending disconnect command. Added load threshold check to AMI disconnect workflow.

title: PHMSA audit finding - incomplete operator qualification records
description: During PHMSA (Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration) audit, inspector found that 12 of 45 field technicians had expired OQ (Operator Qualification) certifications for covered tasks. Technicians had been performing pipeline work with expired qualifications for 1-3 months.
root_cause: The OQ tracking spreadsheet used by the training department had a formula error that was not flagging expirations correctly. The system showed all technicians as current. Additionally, there was no automated notification system - expiration tracking was entirely manual.
resolution: Immediately suspended the 12 technicians from covered tasks. Scheduled emergency re-qualification sessions. Migrated from spreadsheet to dedicated OQ management software (Veriforce). Implemented 60-day and 30-day automated expiration warnings. Added OQ verification step to work order dispatch process. Responded to PHMSA with corrective action plan. All 12 technicians re-qualified within 2 weeks.

title: Gas meter firmware update causing incorrect temperature compensation
description: After firmware v3.2 update, gas meters with built-in temperature compensation are applying incorrect correction factors. Billed volumes are 8-12% higher than actual in warm weather conditions. Affects approximately 5,000 meters.
root_cause: Firmware v3.2 had a unit conversion bug in the temperature compensation algorithm. The code was converting Fahrenheit to Celsius using the formula (F-32)/1.8 but the original algorithm expected Kelvin input. This caused over-correction in temperatures above 60°F.
resolution: Deployed emergency firmware patch v3.2.2 fixing the temperature conversion. Recalculated billing for all 5,000 affected meters for the period between firmware update and patch. Issued credits averaging $34 per customer. Added temperature compensation validation test to firmware QA checklist using known temperature/volume pairs.

title: Voltage regulation issues after large solar farm interconnection
description: Following the interconnection of a 5 MW solar farm on distribution feeder F-112, voltage at the end of the feeder is exceeding 126V (ANSI upper limit) during peak solar production and light load conditions. 340 customers on the feeder.
root_cause: The solar farm's power output during peak production raises the voltage profile along the feeder above the substation regulator's ability to compensate. The feeder was designed for one-way power flow (source to load) and the reverse power flow from the solar farm creates a voltage rise at the point of interconnection.
resolution: Implemented dynamic reactive power control on the solar farm inverters (absorbing VARs during high production). Installed a smart voltage regulator at mid-point of feeder with bidirectional capability. Reconductored 2 miles of feeder to reduce impedance. Voltage now maintained within 120-125V range across all conditions. Updated interconnection study process to include reverse power flow analysis.

title: Customer receiving bill for disconnected gas service
description: Customer moved out 4 months ago and requested service disconnection. Continues to receive monthly gas bills with minimum charges. Customer confirmed meter was turned off by technician. Account still shows as active.
root_cause: Field technician completed the physical disconnection but failed to update the work order status in the mobile application (app crashed during submission). Without the completed work order, the CIS did not process the account closure. The work order remained in 'dispatched' status.
resolution: Closed the account retroactive to the original disconnection date. Waived all charges incurred after disconnection date ($112 in minimum charges). Fixed the work order in the mobile system. Added automatic escalation for work orders in 'dispatched' status for more than 48 hours. Implemented daily reconciliation between field work orders and billing system.

title: Electric meter demand reset not occurring at billing cycle
description: Commercial customer's demand register is not resetting at the end of each billing cycle. Customer showing cumulative peak demand of 450 kW instead of monthly peak of approximately 150 kW. Being billed for highest demand ever recorded rather than current month.
root_cause: Meter demand reset schedule was programmed for the 1st of the month but the customer's billing cycle ends on the 15th. When the CIS was migrated to a new system, the billing cycle date changed but the meter demand reset date was not updated to match.
resolution: Reprogrammed meter demand reset to align with the 15th of the month billing cycle. Manually reset current demand register. Recalculated demand charges for the last 3 billing periods using interval data to determine true monthly peaks. Issued credit of $2,840. Added billing cycle alignment verification to CIS migration checklist.

title: Gas distribution main pressure spike during load reduction
description: Distribution main pressure spiked from 25 PSI to 48 PSI when a large industrial customer unexpectedly shut down, reducing system demand by 30%. District regulator overshoot caused pressure to exceed MAOP of 40 PSI for approximately 8 minutes.
root_cause: District pressure regulator had a slow response time (45 seconds to fully compensate) which is adequate for gradual load changes but insufficient for sudden large demand drops. The regulator was a pilot-operated type with a small pilot orifice that limited response speed.
resolution: Replaced pilot orifice with larger size to improve response time to 12 seconds. Installed monitoring relief valve set at 42 PSI as backup overpressure protection. Added telemetry monitoring for large industrial customer load status. Implemented automated SCADA alarm for pressure approaching 90% of MAOP. Conducted transient analysis study for similar scenarios across the system.

title: Meter data management system rejecting valid interval data
description: MDMS is rejecting approximately 15% of incoming interval data records as 'validation failed'. Rejected records show valid timestamps and reasonable consumption values. Issue started after MDMS software update last weekend.
root_cause: MDMS software update changed the timestamp format validation from accepting both ISO 8601 and Unix epoch formats to only ISO 8601. Approximately 15% of meters (older firmware) still report in Unix epoch format. The validation update was not in the release notes.
resolution: Applied MDMS configuration patch to accept both timestamp formats. Reprocessed all rejected records from the past week (approximately 2.1 million records). All records ingested successfully. Contacted MDMS vendor about undocumented changes. Implemented pre-update validation testing with sample data from all meter firmware versions. Added data ingestion success rate monitoring with alert at <98%.
